definition: The bodily conditioner named in meditative analysis, especially breathing.
notes: Use body conditioner for kāyasaṅkhāra in the breathing tetrad and related practice explanation. The repository treats the term here as the bodily conditioning worked on through breathing, not as a vague body formation label. When the MN 10 / MN 118 practice-text surface is in view, keep this aligned with the governed body-conditioner training line rather than paraphrasing it into breath process or generic bodily activity.

example_phrases.translation: one trains: 'Breathing in, I will calm the body conditioner.'
example_phrases.notes: Body-tetrad calming line in ānāpānasati and the aligned MN 10 breathing section.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'body conditioner' in the governed MN 10 / MN 118 breathing family unless a recorded local reason requires fuller explanation.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not slide back to 'body formation' or loosen the line into generic breath-process language when the practice-text control phrase is in view.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the breathing-family line from drifting into vague bodily-activity or body-formation language.
